MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication On The Complaint Of Milton Garcia And Doris Corado
Off Calendar Jan-24-2020 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Leonard T. Fink, Esq. of SPRINGEL & FINK LLP, T:714.957.5742, for defendant/moving party;
Martin Jerisat, Esq. of SARIOL LEGAL CENTER, T:714.361.8200, for plaintiff/opposing party, on Court Call.

RULING: The tentative ruling was argued. At the conclusion of arguments, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT ALEX CONSTRUCTION & ELECTRIC, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication On The Complaint Of Milton Garcia And Doris Corado, as follows:

Defendant Alex Construction & Electric Inc.'s summary judgment motion is denied. Sued by plaintiff Milton Garcia for negligence, Alex argues that Garcia cannot prove duty, breach or causation and thus that Doris Corado's (Garcia's wife) loss of consortium claim also fails.

Working on a residential roof in San Mateo, Garcia fell from Alex's 28-foot ladder and was injured. Garcia was working for defendant Excellent Technical Roofing Company, Inc.

Alex's motion is largely based on assertions that it was not a general contractor on the job and "did not give Milton permission to use its ladder." Both points are disputed: Excellent's owner declares that "Alex was the general contractor" supervising the job and "knew or should have known" that Excellent provided no equipment for workers, who needed to access the roof and thus had to use Alex's ladder. (Jo Dec. 5-8.)

Order signed.
